title: Pre-identity substrates are healed at ceremony entry — update (and the ceremony family) establishes substrate identity: mint + register + report
context: FGAP-033: mandatory identity stamping (DEC-0012, no lazy mint on write) makes resyncSchema's migrate write throw on a substrate whose config.json carries no substrate_id; the throw is caught as a blocked refusal, so /context update cannot forward-migrate a populated block on a pre-identity substrate, and two S4 migrate-path tests are skipped pending this design. DEC-0012 required the pre-identity behavior to be designed via a dedicated DEC. Empirical grounding: R-0025 (the issue-003 re-drive) confirmed the stamping throw as the refusal's root cause by single-variable reproduction.
decision: Ceremonies establish identity at entry. On a substrate whose config.json lacks a valid substrate_id, the substrate-lifecycle ceremonies (update; the same entry seam the ceremony family shares) mint a substrate_id, register it in the project registry, and report the establishment in the ceremony's result — before the first write that stamps identity. Derived from uniform project precedent, not status-quo preference: every other lagging-state class is HEALED by the sanctioned ceremony (config-version migration decls seeded at every ceremony entry per the TASK-070 class rule; pre-envelope blocks converge on update; catalog-new vocabulary propagates additively on update), and substrate_id minting is already a sanctioned ceremony action (init mints + registers; accept-all preserves-or-mints). DEC-0012's no-lazy-mint rule bans implicit minting inside arbitrary block writes; ceremony-entry establishment is explicit, auth-gated, writer-stamped provisioning at the same trust boundary where init already mints — the rule is preserved, not weakened. Refusal-by-design is rejected: it would make pre-identity the sole lagging-state class the ceremony refuses to heal, contradicting the heal-at-ceremony class rule and best-of-breed operability.
consequences: update on a pre-identity substrate becomes a healing run instead of a mislabeled refusal: identity established at entry (loudly reported in the ceremony result), then the normal drift-routed model update proceeds and the populated-block version bump migrates.

writeBlockForDir's stamping guard stays load-bearing for any write that reaches it without identity (defense in depth); DEC-0012's no-lazy-mint rule is preserved — establishment is explicit ceremony-boundary provisioning, never implicit minting inside a block write.

Implementation lands via a scoped task addressing FGAP-033: the ceremony-entry establishment step, its result reporting, and re-greening (or replacing per this contract) the two skipped S4 migrate-path tests on a substrate that now self-establishes.
FGAP-115's refusal-cause classification remains independently necessary — this decision removes the pre-identity instance from the blocked path, not the conflation class.
